title: pyrus legrei
intro: Pyrus leagi, also known as the wild pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species native to eastern Asia. Its bark, leaves, and fruit possess medicinal properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pyrus leagi include reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and treating digestive issues, as well as its potential in preventing cancer and cardiovascular diseases. Pyrus leagi can be prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine in the form of dried fruit, leaves, or bark extracts.
